title: Rithm Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Youth Leadership Fellowship for Young Changemakers Shaping Human Connection in the Age of AI
summary: Artificial Intelligence is transforming nearly every aspect of modern life—from education and work to communication and relationships. While AI presents exciting opportunities, it also raises important questions about loneliness, digital well-being, and the future of human connection. To ensure young people are not just passive users of technology but active architects of a more connected future, The Rithm Project has opened applications for its Rithm Fellowship 2026–2027 . This paid fellowship is designed for five exceptional young leaders aged 18 to 22 who are passionate about shaping conversations around AI, technology, mental health, education, and human relationships. Fellows will receive a USD $2,000 stipend , work alongside researchers and innovators, contribute to national conversations, and help influence how society approaches human connection in the age of artificial intelligence. About The Rithm Project The Rithm Project is a mission-driven organization dedicated to building a future where human connection thrives alongside artificial intelligence. Rather than viewing AI solely through the lens of productivity or technological advancement, the organization focuses on helping young people cultivate meaningful relationships, empathy, belonging, and community in an increasingly digital world. The organization conducts youth-centered research, develops educational tools and resources, convenes leaders across sectors, and creates opportunities for young people to influence conversations around AI and society. Its vision is to ensure that the next generation helps shape technologies that strengthen rather than weaken human relationships. About the Rithm Fellowship The Rithm Fellowship is a youth leadership programme where participants play an active role in shaping The Rithm Project s work. Rather than simply attending workshops, fellows become trusted collaborators whose perspectives help guide research, influence organizational strategy, and shape products designed for young people. The fellowship intentionally brings together a diverse cohort of five individuals from different backgrounds, experiences, and geographic locations. The goal is to create a community where varied lived experiences contribute to richer conversations about AI, connection, and the future. The fellowship runs from September 2026 through May 2027 , combining leadership development, collaborative project work, public engagement, and opportunities to represent youth voices in national conversations. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive a range of professional and leadership development opportunities, including: USD $2,000 stipend for participation. Opportunity to influence national conversations on AI and human connection. Direct collaboration with researchers, educators, technologists, and mental health experts. Hands-on experience contributing to research and innovative projects. Opportunities to represent youth perspectives at conferences, workshops, and speaking engagements. Leadership development in facilitation, communication, and collaboration. Networking with a multigenerational community of changemakers. Participation in exclusive Rithm Project events and summits. Experience working with one of the leading organizations exploring AI and youth well-being. What Fellows Will Do The fellowship is divided into two primary areas of responsibility. 1. Youth Leadership and Public Representation (Approximately 60%) Fellows will: Represent youth perspectives during internal strategy discussions. Participate in conferences, workshops, and public events. Engage with educators, researchers, policymakers, funders, and mental health professionals. Facilitate discussions that encourage diverse viewpoints. Travel independently within the United States for selected fellowship activities. 2. Research and Project Contributions (Approximately 40%) Participants will also: Contribute to youth-focused research. Pilot educational resources and digital tools. Help shape The Rithm Project s products and initiatives. Share lived experiences that inform future programmes. Collaborate on projects addressing AI, belonging, and social connection. Develop practical ideas that can influence future systems and policies. Who Is the Fellowship Looking For? The Rithm Project is seeking young people who are thoughtful, curious, and willing to engage in meaningful conversations about technology and society. Ideal candidates are individuals who: Have lived experiences or insights related to AI and human connection. Care deeply about strengthening communities and relationships. Are confident sharing ideas while remaining open to different perspectives. Possess empathy and emotional intelligence. Enjoy storytelling and communicating complex ideas in accessible ways. Demonstrate initiative and a willingness to contribute to collaborative projects. Have a growth mindset and are eager to learn from feedback. Can think critically while translating ideas into practical action. Time Commitment The fellowship is intentionally designed to fit alongside academic or professional commitments. Participants should expect to dedicate: Approximately 7–10 hours per month . Additional time for occasional travel and public events. Successful applicants must be available for the following mandatory in-person events in Denver, Colorado: October 16–18, 2026 – Fellowship Kickoff. May 10–13, 2027 – Human Connection and AI Summit. Why You Should Apply The Rithm Fellowship is more than a leadership programme—it is an opportunity to help shape one of the most important conversations of our time. As AI continues to transform education, work, and relationships, young people are uniquely positioned to influence how technology is developed and used. By joining this fellowship, participants will not only develop leadership and communication skills but also contribute to research, public dialogue, and practical solutions that prioritize empathy, connection, and human flourishing. Whether your passion lies in education, technology, mental health, public policy, or community organizing, this fellowship offers a platform to make your voice heard and collaborate with leaders committed to building a healthier digital future. Application Deadline Applications close on 15 July 2026. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early. How to Apply Applications must be submitted through the official Rithm Fellowship webpage . For more information about The Rithm Project and its mission, visit HERE Click here to discover more global fellowship opportunites on OFY. The post Rithm Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Youth Leadership Fellowship for Young Changemakers Shaping Human Connection in the Age of AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship 2026 in Switzerland: Launch Your Global Sustainability Career with the International Olympic Committee
summary: If you are a recent graduate passionate about sustainability, climate action, and creating a positive global impact through sport, the International Olympic Committee (IOC) is offering an exciting opportunity to begin your international career. Applications are now open for the IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship , a 12-month full-time traineeship based in Lausanne, Switzerland . Hosted within the Olympic Games Department , this internship provides young professionals with the opportunity to contribute to sustainability initiatives that support one of the world s most recognized international organizations. As the guardian of the Olympic Games and the leader of the Olympic Movement, the IOC works with stakeholders across the globe to promote sustainable development, environmental responsibility, and social impact through sport. Successful candidates will work alongside experienced sustainability professionals while contributing to projects related to climate action, biodiversity, circular economy practices, sustainable procurement, and international partnerships. This is an outstanding opportunity for graduates seeking practical experience within a multicultural environment while helping shape the future of sustainable sport. About the International Olympic Committee The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is an independent international organization responsible for overseeing the Olympic Games and promoting Olympic values worldwide. Beyond organizing the world s largest sporting event, the IOC collaborates with governments, international organizations, National Olympic Committees, International Federations, athletes, and global partners to promote peace, inclusion, education, sustainability, and development through sport. The IOC maintains partnerships with organizations such as: United Nations (UN) United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) International Federations National Olympic Committees Worldwide Olympic Partners Working at the IOC offers employees exposure to a truly international environment with colleagues representing more than 70 nationalities , making it one of the most diverse organizations in global sport. About the Sustainability Trainee Role The Sustainability Trainee will become part of the IOC Sustainability Team within the Olympic Games Department and will support the implementation of projects aligned with the IOC Sustainability Strategy. The internship provides hands-on experience in planning, coordinating, monitoring, and delivering sustainability initiatives that influence not only the IOC as an organization but also the Olympic Games and the wider Olympic Movement. This role is ideal for graduates who wish to combine their passion for environmental sustainability with sport, international development, and project management. Key Responsibilities As a Sustainability Trainee, you will support numerous sustainability initiatives across different areas. Your responsibilities will include: Climate and Environmental Sustainability Support projects related to climate action and climate adaptation. Contribute to biodiversity initiatives. Assist with sustainable procurement activities. Support circular economy projects across the Olympic Movement. Partnership Support Collaborate with sustainability partnerships including: Sport for Climate Action Framework Sport for Nature Framework Event Coordination Assist in organizing sustainability-related meetings and events involving: International Federations National Olympic Committees IOC Commercial Partners UNEP IUCN UNFCCC Other institutional partners Tasks may include: Preparing invitations Coordinating logistics Creating presentations Facilitating meetings Following up on action items Reporting and Project Monitoring The trainee will also: Prepare sustainability reports. Develop dashboards measuring sustainability progress. Support carbon footprint reporting. Assist with procurement sustainability reporting. Project Management The successful candidate will help coordinate flagship initiatives including: IOC Climate Action Awards Athlete Sustainability Ambassador Programme In addition, the trainee will provide support for cross-functional sustainability projects across various IOC departments. What You Will Gain Working at the IOC provides far more than technical experience. Benefits include: Twelve months of international professional experience. Opportunity to work at IOC headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland. Exposure to global sustainability strategies. Practical experience managing international projects. Collaboration with leading sustainability experts. Experience working with United Nations agencies. Networking with professionals from more than 70 countries. Flexible and rewarding work environment. Competitive employee benefits. Health and wellness initiatives. Opportunity to contribute to global climate action through sport. Professional growth within one of the world s most prestigious international organizations. Qualifications Applicants should possess: A Bachelor s degree (minimum) in Sustainability, Sustainable Development, Environmental Studies, or a related discipline. Approximately one year of professional experience in sustainability or a similar field. Fluency in English. French or another language is considered an advantage. Strong Microsoft 365 skills including: Word Excel PowerPoint Outlook Understanding of sustainability principles. Knowledge of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Familiarity with LinkedIn and other social media platforms. Desired Skills and Personal Attributes The IOC is looking for candidates who demonstrate: Reliability and professionalism. Excellent planning and organizational abilities. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Critical thinking. Initiative and proactive problem-solving. Ability to prioritize multiple assignments. Excellent teamwork skills. Respect for diversity and inclusion. Commitment to ethical conduct. Passion for sustainability and the Olympic values. Why You Should Apply The IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship is one of the most prestigious early-career opportunities available in sustainability and international sport. It offers graduates the chance to work on projects with global environmental significance while collaborating with leading organizations such as UNEP and UNFCCC. Beyond gaining practical experience, trainees become part of the Olympic Movement, helping deliver initiatives that contribute to climate action, biodiversity conservation, sustainable procurement, and environmental leadership across international sport. Whether your long-term ambition is to work with international organizations, the United Nations, NGOs, governments, or sustainability consulting firms, this traineeship provides exceptional exposure and career-building opportunities. Application Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the high level of international competition. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official International Olympic Committee careers portal . Learn more about this internship opportunity here Click here to discover more global internship opportunities on OFY. The post IOC Sustainability Trainee Internship 2026 in Switzerland: Launch Your Global Sustainability Career with the International Olympic Committee appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Talent Pipeline Fly-In Grant Program 2026: Up to $25,000 for North Dakota Communities to Attract Skilled Workers
summary: North Dakota is tackling one of its toughest workforce challenges head-on with the Talent Pipeline Fly-In Grant Program . Administered by the North Dakota Department of Commerce s Workforce Development Division, the program offers eligible communities up to $25,000 in matching grant funding to host fly-in events that introduce qualified out-of-state job seekers to local career opportunities — and to the quality of life that comes with them. Instead of relying only on traditional recruitment channels, the program lets communities bring prospective workers to North Dakota to see it for themselves: meeting employers, touring neighborhoods, exploring local amenities, and getting a real feel for why the state has become an attractive place to build a career and a life. Applications are open now , with funding awarded on a rolling basis until all available funds are committed. About the Program The Fly-In Grant Program was created in response to North Dakota s persistent labor shortage. The 2025 North Dakota Workforce Ecosystem Assessment found that employers rank a lack of available workers as their top recruitment challenge and despite one of the lowest unemployment rates in the country, the state still has more than 16,000 open jobs. The Department of Commerce recognized that a job posting alone rarely convinces someone to relocate. The Fly-In Program was designed to close that gap by giving prospective employees a firsthand, immersive introduction to North Dakota communities. Through these events, participants can: Meet directly with hiring employers Explore available career opportunities Tour neighborhoods Learn about local schools and housing Experience community attractions and amenities Build relationships with community members Get a genuine sense of everyday life in North Dakota before deciding to relocate The goal is simple: reduce the uncertainty of relocating while giving employers direct access to motivated, qualified candidates. Why It Matters Lifestyle factors play a major role in where people choose to live and work. The 2025 Light cast Talent Attraction Scorecard identifies work-life balance as one of the top considerations for job seekers evaluating new opportunities. Relocation research also points to recurring concerns for people considering a move, including: Finding suitable housing Adjusting to a new environment Accessing community services Building social connections Understanding local schools and infrastructure Evaluating commutes and neighborhoods By letting prospective residents experience these things in person before committing, communities can build confidence in the decision to relocate and improve retention once people arrive. Proven in the Pilot Before scaling statewide, North Dakota piloted the concept with a Fly-In event in Grand Forks in 2023. Of the seven participants: Two relocated to North Dakota , bringing an additional working adult, one child, and one teenager with them Two more continued pursuing relocation with their families The event generated valuable insight into what participants expect, what activities resonate most, and what information matters most during relocation planning That pilot s success laid the groundwork for expanding the program to communities across the state. Funding Available Up to $25,000 in grant funding per approved Fly-In event A minimum 1:1 matching contribution is required from the applicant Communities may apply for one Fly-In event at a time Funding is available until all allocated resources are obligated Program Objectives The Fly-In Grant Program is designed to: Recruit qualified out-of-state job seekers Address workforce shortages across North Dakota Strengthen employer recruitment efforts Showcase the state s quality of life Encourage permanent relocation Build stronger employer-employee relationships Increase long-term workforce retention Support sustainable economic development Ultimately, the program is measured on one outcome above all: converting event participants into permanent North Dakota residents. What a Fly-In Event Must Include Approved events should combine real employment opportunities with genuine community engagement. At minimum, applicants must include: Employer participation from one to three major local industries Direct interaction between participants and hiring employers Community tours Information on housing, schools, and local services Recreational and networking activities Additional experiences that showcase life in North Dakota Grantees must also commit to staying in contact with participants for up to one year after the event to track relocation outcomes. Eligible Expenses Matching Requirement Grant funds may be used for approved expenses tied to planning and hosting the Fly-In event, provided they comply with program requirements and the approved grant agreement. Recipients must keep complete financial documentation throughout the project. Matching contributions equal to the grant amount can include: Cash contributions Donations Discounts Approved in-kind contributions Matching resources should be clearly identified in the proposal. How to Apply Applications are submitted through the official online grant portal . Portal access approval can take up to three business days — request access well before your intended submission date Late access requests will not extend your application timeline Proposals must follow the required format outlined in the program guidelines; applications with formatting issues may be returned for correction APPLY HERE How Applications Are Evaluated The Workforce Development Division reviews applications based on: Current community efforts to attract new residents Demonstrated recruitment strategies Employer participation Planned Fly-In activities Availability of required matching funds Overall program readiness Applications are reviewed in the order they are received . Visit HERE to learn more about Talent Pipeline Fly-In Grant Program 2026 Review Timeline Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis in the order they are received. Applicants can generally expect clarification requests within three weeks of submission, while award notifications are typically issued within four weeks . Funding will remain available until all allocated resources have been committed. Successful applicants will receive details of their award, designated contact requirements, and instructions for finalizing the grant agreement. Reporting and Compliance Grant recipients are required to submit ongoing performance reports, a mid-project progress narrative, and a final report within 15 days of project completion or by June 30, 2027 , whichever comes first. Final reports must outline key outcomes, including job offers made and accepted, participant relocations, employer feedback, project costs, lessons learned, and a detailed breakdown of grant and matching fund expenditures. The Department of Commerce may also request follow-up updates for up to one year after the event. Grant Payments Funding is distributed in two phases: 50% upon execution of the grant agreement. 50% after approval of the final report and verification of eligible expenditures. Recipients must maintain complete financial records and supporting documentation throughout the project period. Key Dates Applications Open: March 24, 2026 Application Status: Currently Open Funding Allocation: Rolling basis until funds are fully committed Fly-In Events Deadline: June 30, 2027 Final Report Deadline: Within 15 days of project completion or by June 30, 2027, whichever comes first. Conclusion The Talent Pipeline Fly-In Grant Program offers North Dakota communities up to $25,000 in matching grant funding to attract skilled out-of-state professionals through immersive recruitment events. By connecting job seekers with employers and showcasing the state s quality of life, the program aims to strengthen local workforces and support long-term economic growth. Eligible applicants are encouraged to apply early, as funding is awarded on a rolling basis until all available funds are committed. For more global grant opportunities visit OFY HERE The post Talent Pipeline Fly-In Grant Program 2026: Up to $25,000 for North Dakota Communities to Attract Skilled Workers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: KWI International Fellowships 2027 – A Prestigious Research Opportunity in Germany
summary: The Institute for Advanced Study in the Humanities (KWI) in Essen, Germany, has officially opened applications for the KWI International Fellowships 2027 . This highly regarded program offers outstanding researchers in the humanities, cultural studies, and social sciences the chance to engage in independent, innovative research while benefiting from KWI’s interdisciplinary environment. Fellowship Duration and Deadline Program Dates: 1 April 2027 – 30 September 2027 Application Deadline: 17 August 2026 (midnight, CET) Applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their materials well in advance to ensure compliance with the strict deadline. About KWI – Institute for Advanced Study in the Humanities Located in the Ruhr Area, one of Europe’s largest metropolitan regions, KWI is an interdisciplinary research institute dedicated to advancing knowledge in the humanities and social sciences. The institute emphasizes: Science studies Visual literacy Aesthetic practices Constellations of historical impact Dialogues between cultural and social sciences KWI also hosts the Academy in Exile , providing research space for scholars whose academic freedom is under threat. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will enjoy a range of benefits designed to support their research and professional growth: Financial Support Monthly allowance of €3,000 (pre‑tax) to cover rent, insurance, and living expenses. Partial reimbursement of travel expenses for arrival and departure. Research Infrastructure Access to office space, technical support, and library services. Event and research management assistance. Administrative and communication support. Academic Engagement Participation in colloquia, lectures, conferences, and reading groups. Contribution to the KWI Blog . Opportunity to organize a workshop (depending on resources and scheduling). Collaborative Opportunities Networking within the University Alliance Ruhr (UA Ruhr) , which links the universities of Duisburg‑Essen, Bochum, and Dortmund. Potential collaborations with nearby universities in Düsseldorf, Cologne, Münster, and Bielefeld. Cross‑border initiatives with Belgium and the Netherlands. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Hold a PhD in the humanities or social sciences . Have up to six years of postdoctoral experience (PhD completed in 2021 or later). Career breaks (e.g., care responsibilities or scholar‑at‑risk status) may be explained in the cover letter and will be considered. Open to researchers worldwide. German nationals may apply only if employed at an international institution. Applications must be submitted individually (no group or team applications). Expectations of Fellows During the fellowship, participants are expected to: Dedicate their time to research aligned with KWI’s programmatic agenda. Be present at least three days per week at the institute. Participate in entry and exit interviews. Actively engage in academic events and contribute to the intellectual community. Mention KWI in publications resulting from the fellowship. Begin the fellowship on the announced start date (1 April 2027). Application Process The application must include: Curriculum Vitae (CV) List of publications PhD certificate Project proposal (up to 5,000 characters) Important notes: Applications must be in English. Incomplete or late submissions will not be considered. Proposals exceeding the specified length will be disqualified. Selection will be made by a committee comprising members of the KWI governing board and the institute’s director. Applications must be submitted electronically as a single PDF file (max. 20 MB) to: international.fellowship@uni-due.de The Ruhr Area – A Cultural Hub The Ruhr Area, with over five million inhabitants, is one of Europe’s largest metropolitan regions. Essen, recognized as the European Capital of Culture in 2010 , offers: A diverse urban landscape with cultural appeal. Green spaces developed after the transformation of traditional industries. Easy access to Cologne, Düsseldorf, Belgium, and the Netherlands for day trips. This vibrant environment provides fellows with both academic and cultural enrichment during their stay. Visit HERE for more information about the KWI International Fellowships 2027. Conclusion The KWI International Fellowships 2027 represent a unique opportunity for researchers in the humanities, cultural studies, and social sciences to advance their work in a supportive, interdisciplinary environment. With generous financial support, access to world‑class infrastructure, and opportunities for collaboration across Europe, this fellowship is an ideal platform for scholars seeking to make a meaningful impact in their fields. Application Deadline: 17 August 2026 Interested candidates should prepare their applications carefully and submit them on time to secure consideration for this prestigious program. For more Global Fellowship Opportunities, visit OFY HERE The post KWI International Fellowships 2027 – A Prestigious Research Opportunity in Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women’s Climate Leadership Programme by Wedu 2026: Empowering Emerging Women Leaders Across South and Southeast Asia to Drive Climate Resilience
summary: Climate change is one of the defining challenges of our time, affecting millions of people across the world. While women are among those most impacted by climate-related disasters, they are also leading innovative solutions within their communities. Despite their contributions, women remain significantly underrepresented in climate leadership and decision-making spaces. Recognising this leadership gap, Wedu has made Women s Climate Leadership one of its strategic priorities in 2025. Through an integrated ecosystem of leadership development, education financing, and mentorship, Wedu is creating opportunities for women across South and Southeast Asia to become influential climate leaders capable of driving sustainable change. At the centre of this initiative is the Women Rising for Climate Resilience Programme , an eight-week online leadership programme specifically designed to equip emerging women leaders with the knowledge, skills, networks, and resources needed to create lasting climate solutions within their communities. This initiative is not simply another leadership programme. It represents a long-term movement dedicated to strengthening women s participation in climate action across Asia. About Wedu s Women s Climate Leadership Initiative Wedu supports women throughout different stages of their leadership journey by combining three interconnected pillars that create a complete ecosystem for growth. These pillars include: Leadership Development Education Financing Mentorship Rather than focusing on only one aspect of leadership, Wedu provides continuous support that enables women to build their capabilities, access financial resources, and connect with experienced mentors and peers. This holistic model ensures participants receive support from the beginning of their leadership journey through to becoming established changemakers capable of influencing climate policy, community resilience, and environmental sustainability. Why Women s Climate Leadership Matters Now Across South and Southeast Asia, women are already making meaningful contributions to climate action. Many women are: Leading community resilience initiatives. Developing locally driven environmental solutions. Supporting sustainable livelihoods. Advocating for climate justice. Organising disaster preparedness programmes. Promoting sustainable agricultural practices. Driving grassroots environmental movements. Despite these contributions, women continue to face significant barriers to leadership and decision-making. Current statistics highlight this imbalance: Women represent only 30–35% of Asian delegations participating in international climate negotiations. In South Asia, women account for only 16.6% of parliamentarians. This underrepresentation affects climate governance and policy development. Research conducted across 91 countries has demonstrated that higher levels of female representation in leadership are associated with: Stronger climate policies. Improved environmental governance. Lower carbon emissions. More inclusive decision-making. Wedu believes climate solutions become stronger when women have equal opportunities to lead. The Three Pillars of Wedu s Climate Leadership Movement Wedu s Women s Climate Leadership initiative is built around three essential pillars that collectively empower women to become effective climate leaders. Capability The programme focuses on developing leadership skills necessary for addressing climate challenges. Participants strengthen their understanding of: Climate justice Systems thinking Leadership strategies Community engagement Collaborative problem-solving Climate policy Sustainable development Social innovation These competencies enable participants to create practical and locally relevant climate solutions. Capital Innovative ideas often require financial support to become reality. Where resources are available, graduates may receive: Access to seed funding Support for pilot climate initiatives Opportunities to transform project ideas into real community solutions Financial support helps emerging leaders move from planning to implementation. Community Leadership thrives through strong networks. Participants become part of a vibrant community that includes: Women climate leaders Mentors Climate organisations Wedu alumni Peer learning circles Regional collaborators This community provides ongoing encouragement, partnerships, and knowledge-sharing beyond programme completion. Interested applicant should APPLY HERE Women Rising for Climate Resilience Programme The flagship programme under Wedu s climate initiative is the Women Rising for Climate Resilience Programme . Designed specifically for emerging women leaders across South and Southeast Asia, this programme combines leadership education with practical climate action. Participants engage in: Eight weeks of structured online learning. Interactive leadership development sessions. Collaborative learning experiences. Practical climate problem-solving. Peer discussions. Expert guidance. The curriculum has been carefully co-designed with experienced women climate leaders and climate organisations throughout the region. Rather than relying solely on theory, the programme encourages participants to apply learning directly to real challenges within their own communities. What Participants Will Learn Throughout the programme, participants explore a wide range of leadership and climate-related topics. Learning areas include: Climate justice frameworks. Systems thinking. Adaptive leadership. Community-centred climate solutions. Environmental resilience. Sustainable development approaches. Leadership communication. Strategic planning. Collaboration and partnership building. Practical project development. The programme ensures that participants leave with actionable knowledge rather than theoretical understanding alone. Climate Solution Concept Note An important component of the programme involves developing a Climate Solution Concept Note . Participants identify climate-related challenges affecting their communities and develop practical proposals for addressing those issues. These concept notes serve as the foundation for future climate initiatives and community projects. Following programme completion, graduates continue receiving mentorship to refine and implement their ideas. Mentorship Beyond the Programme Unlike many short-term training programmes, Wedu continues supporting participants after graduation. Graduates receive ongoing mentorship designed to help them: Refine project ideas. Navigate implementation challenges. Strengthen leadership confidence. Build partnerships. Expand community impact. Develop long-term climate initiatives. This continued guidance increases the likelihood that participants projects will create meaningful and lasting change. Opportunities for Seed Funding Where funding resources are available, selected graduates may gain access to seed funding to pilot their climate initiatives. Seed funding can support: Community resilience projects. Environmental education programmes. Climate adaptation initiatives. Sustainable livelihood projects. Local environmental innovations. This financial support helps participants transition from ideas to measurable impact. Join a Regional Network of Climate Leaders Graduates become members of Wedu s growing network of women leaders. Participants gain access to: Wedu Alumni Network. Peer learning circles. Climate Alliance for Women Leaders in Asia (CAWLA). Networking opportunities. Regional collaboration. Leadership development resources. These networks create opportunities for lifelong collaboration and continued professional growth. Climate Alliance for Women Leaders in Asia (CAWLA) One of the initiative s most significant strengths is its connection with the Climate Alliance for Women Leaders in Asia (CAWLA). CAWLA was established by women climate leaders and climate organisations seeking to create stronger regional collaboration. The alliance provides: Peer support. Shared learning opportunities. Leadership collaboration. Regional networking. Collective advocacy. By connecting participants with CAWLA, Wedu ensures graduates remain engaged in a broader climate leadership movement. Built by Women Climate Leaders A defining feature of the programme is that it was not created for women—it was created with women. The curriculum was co-designed alongside: Women climate leaders. Climate organisations. Regional experts. Community practitioners. Their lived experiences, knowledge, and priorities shaped every aspect of the programme. This collaborative design ensures the learning experience reflects real-world challenges and practical leadership needs across South and Southeast Asia. VISIT here to learn more about the Women s Climate Leadership Programme by Wedu 2026 Wedu s Long-Term Vision Wedu has established an ambitious target for its climate leadership initiative. Target by 2029: Enable 100 women to successfully complete the Women Rising for Climate Resilience Programme . By investing in emerging leaders today, Wedu hopes to strengthen climate resilience across communities throughout Asia for years to come. Benefits of Joining the Programme Participants can expect numerous personal and professional development opportunities, including: Eight weeks of structured online leadership training. Climate justice education. Systems thinking skills. Leadership development. Practical climate project design. Ongoing mentorship. Potential access to seed funding. Membership in Wedu s alumni community. Regional networking opportunities. Connection with CAWLA. Lifelong peer support. Opportunities for collaborative climate action. Why This Programme Stands Out Unlike conventional leadership programmes, Wedu s initiative combines education, mentorship, financing, and long-term community support into one integrated ecosystem. Its distinguishing features include: Women-led programme design. Region-specific climate leadership focus. Practical application of learning. Continued mentorship after graduation. Opportunities for project implementation. Strong regional partnerships. Long-term professional network. These features ensure participants continue growing long after completing the programme. Conclusion The Women Rising for Climate Resilience Programme represents an important investment in the future of climate leadership across South and Southeast Asia. By combining leadership development, mentorship, education financing, regional collaboration, and opportunities for practical implementation, Wedu is equipping emerging women leaders with the tools needed to address some of the most pressing environmental challenges facing their communities. Rather than simply preparing participants to understand climate change, the programme empowers them to lead meaningful action, influence decision-making, and build resilient communities. As Wedu works toward its goal of supporting 100 women leaders by 2029, this initiative has the potential to strengthen climate leadership across the region and inspire lasting environmental and social impact. Application Deadline: No application deadline has been specified in the provided information. Applicants are encourgaed to apply early. For more global FELLOWSHIP opportunities view HERE The post Women s Climate Leadership Programme by Wedu 2026: Empowering Emerging Women Leaders Across South and Southeast Asia to Drive Climate Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: IFAD Announces US$1.2 Million Grant Call for Proposals to Strengthen Rural Development Policies Worldwide
summary: Organizations working in rural development, policy research, and agricultural investment now have an exciting opportunity to secure funding through the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) . IFAD has launched a Call for Proposals for a grant of up to US$1.2 million to implement the initiative Leveraging IFAD s Rural Sector Performance Assessment (RSPA) for Policy and Investment. The initiative aims to strengthen rural policy assessment and improve the policy and investment environment for sustainable rural development across IFAD partner countries. Grant Overview Funding Amount: Up to US$1.2 million Funding Organization: International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) Programme: Leveraging IFAD s Rural Sector Performance Assessment (RSPA) for Policy and Investment Application Deadline: 4 September 2026 (12:00 CET) Expression of Interest Deadline (Optional): 31 July 2026 This call for proposals is to select a recipient or consortium of recipients to receive an IFAD grant financing to implement the initiative: L everaging IFAD’s Rural Sector Performance Assessment for Policy and Investment for a total amount of up to US$1.2 million. IFAD has identified enhanced policy assessment as a priority area for support to improve the country-level policy and investment environment for rural development (see attached Grant Identification Note for details). The competitively selected recipient can be a non-profit organization, private sector company, university, think tank or research institute. The selected grant recipient will be required to provide a counterpart contribution of no less than 20% of the IFAD grant amount. In the case of private sector institutions, the counterpart contribution shall be no less than 25% of the IFAD grant amount. In-kind contributions may be taken into consideration. Potential applicants are encouraged to send Expressions of interest to RSPAgrant@ifad.org by 31 July 2026. These are not mandatory and non-binding. The deadline to receive proposals is 4 September 2026, 12:00 (CET) How to submit a proposal Send the following documents to RSPAgrant@ifad.org with “CFP RSPA Grant application” in the subject. All are mandatory for the proposal to be evaluated. Project proposal using the Grant Concept Note Template (item 2). The proposal must be aligned with the Grant Identification Note (item 1), specifying the objectives, scope of work, deliverables, and indicative financial envelope. Indicative budget using the activity-based budget template (item 6). Bidders self-certification of eligibility for IFAD grant financing using the template in item 3 (3-1 or 3-2). For this, applicants must read and accept the Grant Design Document template (item 10). The Grant Design Document will be developed by the selected organization at a later stage. Brief institutional profile, detailing experience in the priority area and specific theme outlined in the Grant Concept Note, and in the region/country(ies) where the grant would be implemented – as detailed in the Grant Identification Note Please refer to the “Resources for Applying” section for detailed information and consult the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) document for additional guidance. Deadline to receive proposals is 4 September 2026, 12:00 hours (CET). The RSPA Grant team will respond to queries sent to RSPAgrant@ifad.org Online Q A session The session objective is to address any queries related to this Call for Proposal Date: 21 July 2026 Time: 14:00 to 15:00 (CET) Where: Join here Resources for applying Item 1. The Grant Identification Note Item 2. Grant Concept Note Template Item 3.1 Self certification of eligibility for IFAD grant financing non-UN entities Item 3.2 Self certification of eligibility for IFAD grant financing UN entities Item 4. title: African Union (AU) Internship Program 2026 | Apply for the AU Internship Opportunity
summary: Are you a student or recent graduate looking to gain international work experience with one of Africa s leading intergovernmental organizations? The African Union (AU) Internship Program 2026 is now open for applications from qualified nationals of AU Member States. The internship provides talented young Africans with an opportunity to gain practical experience, develop professional skills, and contribute to the work of the African Union Commission (AUC) while working in a multicultural environment. About the African Union Internship Program The African Union Internship Program is a full-time professional development opportunity designed for students and recent graduates from diverse academic disciplines. The program offers participants hands-on experience across various AU departments while helping them strengthen their technical, leadership, and intercultural skills. The internship also serves as a platform for nurturing the next generation of African leaders committed to advancing sustainable development, peace, governance, and regional integration across the continent. Benefits of the AU Internship Program Successful interns will: Gain valuable professional experience within the African Union. Work alongside experienced international professionals. Build practical skills in policy, administration, research, communications, and project management. Develop leadership and intercultural competencies. Expand their professional network across Africa. Contribute to continental development initiatives. Departments Hosting Interns Internship opportunities are available across numerous AU departments, including: Office of the Chairperson and Deputy Chairperson Economic Development, Trade, Industry Mining (ETIM) Education, Science, Technology Innovation (ESTI) Political Affairs, Peace Security (PAPS) Health, Humanitarian Affairs Social Development (HHS) Infrastructure Energy (IE) Human Resources Management Strategic Planning Delivery Women, Gender Youth (WGY) Information Communication New Partnership for Africa s Development (NEPAD) Other AU Directorates and Specialized Units African Union Internship Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be nationals of an African Union Member State . Be aged between 22 and 32 years at the time of application. Be enrolled in the final year of a Bachelor s degree , pursuing postgraduate studies, or be a recent graduate from a recognized institution. Be fluent in at least one AU official working language: English French Arabic Portuguese Kiswahili Demonstrate integrity, professionalism, and strong interpersonal skills. Meet any additional requirements outlined in the internship Terms of Reference. Required Skills Ideal candidates should possess: Strong written and verbal communication skills. Good teamwork and interpersonal abilities. Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and email applications. Analytical and problem-solving skills. Knowledge of an additional AU language is considered an advantage. Internship Duration The African Union Internship lasts: Initial duration: 3 months Extension: Up to an additional 3 months based on performance and departmental needs. Interns are expected to work full-time: Monday to Friday 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Financial Support Applicants should note that the African Union does not provide financial assistance for: Travel expenses Accommodation Living expenses Per diem Medical insurance Successful interns are responsible for arranging and covering all personal costs throughout the internship. The AU may provide reasonable assistance with visa processing where applicable. Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare the following documents: Motivation letter explaining what they hope to gain from the internship Copy of a valid passport or national identity card Certified academic certificates Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) Recommendation letter from their university or institution Applicants are also required to complete their profile using the official African Union CV Template available on the application portal. How to Apply for the African Union Internship Program To apply: Visit the African Union Careers Portal . All internship applications MUST be submitted through the AU Careers portal at: https://careers.au.int Create an online applicant profile. Complete the online application form. Upload all required supporting documents. Submit your application before the deadline. After submission, applicants will receive an email confirming successful receipt of their application. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Who can apply? The internship is open to nationals of African Union Member States who are final-year undergraduate students, postgraduate students, or recent graduates. Is the internship paid? No. The African Union does not provide a salary, stipend, travel funding, accommodation, or living allowance. How long is the internship? The internship runs for an initial period of three months and may be extended for another three months based on performance. Will I receive confirmation after applying? Yes. Applicants receive an automatic email confirmation once their online application has been successfully submitted. Can I track my application? Yes. Applicants can monitor the status of their application through the AU Careers Portal. What kind of work will interns perform? Assignments vary depending on the department or unit where the intern is placed and may include research, administration, communications, project support, policy analysis, and technical assistance. Can I take leave during the internship? Leave may be granted only in exceptional circumstances with approval from the supervisor, provided it does not affect the internship assignment. What if I experience technical issues during the application? Applicants experiencing technical problems should contact the African Union Internship Team via the official support email provided on the Careers Portal. Why Apply for the AU Internship? The African Union Internship Program is one of Africa s most prestigious early-career opportunities. It allows students and graduates to gain international exposure, strengthen their professional competencies, and contribute directly to initiatives that promote peace, development, governance, economic integration, health, education, and youth empowerment across the continent. Whether you aspire to work in international development, diplomacy, public policy, governance, or humanitarian affairs, the AU Internship offers an excellent platform to launch your career. Apply for the African Union Internship Program Interested candidates should submit their applications through the African Union Careers Portal and ensure all required documents are uploaded before the application deadline. Visit the African Union Careers Portal . All internship applications MUST be submitted through the AU Careers portal at: https://careers.au.int For further inquiries: Internship@africa-union.org The post African Union (AU) Internship Program 2026 | Apply for the AU Internship Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Stellenbosch University MSc Conservation Ecology Bursary 2027: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for South African Students to Advance Free-Flowing River Conservation Across the SADC Region
summary: Applications are now open for the Stellenbosch University MSc Conservation Ecology Bursary 2027 , an exciting fully funded master s opportunity for South African graduates interested in ecology, hydrology, environmental science, and freshwater conservation. The bursary is offered under the Water Research Commission (WRC) project titled Updating and Prioritising the Inventory of Free-Flowing Rivers in the SADC Region. This prestigious bursary provides successful applicants with full university fee coverage and a monthly stipend while pursuing a full-time Master of Science (MSc) in Conservation Ecology at Stellenbosch University during the 2027 academic year. The research contributes directly to one of Southern Africa s most significant freshwater conservation initiatives by developing an updated inventory of Free-Flowing Rivers (FFRs) across the Southern African Development Community (SADC). The project combines ecological science, hydrology, environmental policy, and geospatial analysis to support biodiversity conservation and sustainable water resource management throughout the region. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, with the final application deadline set for 31 August 2026 . About the MSc Conservation Ecology Bursary The MSc bursary forms part of a major regional research initiative funded by the Water Research Commission (WRC) . The project seeks to improve scientific understanding and conservation of Free-Flowing Rivers (FFRs) throughout Southern Africa by developing a regionally appropriate inventory and assessment framework. The successful master s student will become part of an interdisciplinary research team contributing to high-impact environmental research that supports conservation planning, biodiversity protection, and sustainable freshwater management across the SADC region. The research project will be implemented by Southern Waters Ecological Research and Consulting , an environmental consultancy specializing in freshwater ecosystems, ecological assessment, and river conservation. Background of the Research Project Free-Flowing Rivers represent some of the world s most valuable freshwater ecosystems. These rivers provide numerous ecological, economic, and social benefits, including: Maintaining freshwater biodiversity Supporting fisheries Sustaining local livelihoods Preserving river connectivity Supporting sediment transport Maintaining ecosystem functioning Enhancing climate resilience Protecting aquatic habitats Despite their importance, many rivers throughout Southern Africa have experienced increasing pressures from: Infrastructure development River fragmentation Water abstraction Pollution Land degradation Climate change Unsustainable resource use Although international environmental frameworks increasingly recognize the importance of protecting Free-Flowing Rivers, conservation efforts within the SADC region remain constrained by the absence of: Updated regional inventories Consistent definitions Spatial datasets Monitoring frameworks Region-specific ecological assessment methods The Water Research Commission project seeks to address these knowledge gaps. Purpose of the Water Research Commission Project The project aims to create an updated and scientifically robust inventory of Free-Flowing Rivers across the Southern African Development Community. Major objectives include: Developing a regional definition of Free-Flowing Rivers for the SADC region. Updating spatial inventories of river systems. Assessing river connectivity. Evaluating ecological degradation. Identifying restoration opportunities. Supporting biodiversity conservation planning. Aligning river conservation efforts with international biodiversity commitments. The project builds upon methodologies previously developed through the Africa s Living Rivers Programme while adapting them specifically for Southern African river systems. Research Focus of the MSc Student The selected MSc candidate will undertake original research contributing directly to the project s objectives. Key research activities include: 1. Defining Free-Flowing Rivers The research will explore various environmental flow concepts to develop an appropriate definition of Free-Flowing Rivers for Southern Africa. Areas of investigation include: Hydrological thresholds Sediment transport River connectivity Migratory fish species Ecological functioning Human pressures on freshwater ecosystems 2. Comparative Analysis The student will compare newly developed regional definitions with existing international geospatial methodologies. This includes evaluating approaches such as those developed by Grill et al. (2019) and determining their applicability within the SADC context. 3. River Inventory Development The successful applicant will generate updated inventories of Free-Flowing Rivers using ecological and geospatial datasets. This work will contribute directly to regional conservation planning and environmental decision-making. Importance of the Research The research has significant implications for environmental conservation across Southern Africa. Expected impacts include: Supporting biodiversity conservation. Improving freshwater ecosystem management. Strengthening river conservation policies. Informing environmental safeguards. Supporting sustainable development. Protecting aquatic biodiversity. Guiding restoration initiatives. Contributing to international biodiversity reporting. The project also supports implementation of the Global Biodiversity Framework , particularly: Target 2 Target 3 These targets focus on ecosystem restoration and effective conservation of biodiversity. Financial Support The bursary provides comprehensive financial assistance throughout the master s programme. Funding includes: Full university tuition fees. Monthly living stipend during 2027. If additional time is required to complete the degree, limited financial support for: University registration. A limited stipend during 2028 may be available. Duration of the Programme The bursary supports: Registration during the 2027 academic year . One-year MSc study period. Where necessary, limited support may extend into 2028 to facilitate completion of the research. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements. Candidates should possess: A BSc Honours degree or equivalent qualification. Academic background in: Ecology Hydrology Water Science Environmental Science Conservation Ecology Related disciplines Additional requirements include: Minimum average mark of 60% in the major subject. Strong academic record. High motivation for research. Excellent communication skills. Willingness to study full-time. South African citizenship. Preferred Qualifications Although not compulsory, preference will be given to applicants with: GIS experience. Geospatial analysis skills. Environmental modelling experience. Freshwater ecology knowledge. Research experience. Data analysis skills. Candidates demonstrating strong quantitative and analytical abilities may be particularly competitive. Skills and Attributes The project seeks applicants who demonstrate: Scientific curiosity. Research motivation. Independent thinking. Problem-solving skills. Strong written communication. Teamwork. Commitment to environmental conservation. Interest in freshwater ecosystems. Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package containing: Short motivation letter. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV). At least two recent contactable referees. Certified academic transcripts. Certified academic certificates. Incomplete applications may not be considered. How to Apply Applications should be submitted via email. Email: hassan@southernwaters.co.za Email Subject: WRC-FFR Masters Bursary Application Applicants should ensure all required documents are attached before submission. Application Timeline Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis . Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Final Application Deadline: 31 August 2026 Late applications may not be considered. Why You Should Apply This bursary offers an exceptional opportunity for graduates interested in freshwater conservation, ecology, and environmental research. Successful candidates will benefit from: Fully funded MSc study. Monthly financial support. Research addressing regional conservation priorities. Experience in advanced ecological research. Training in geospatial analysis. Opportunities to contribute to international biodiversity goals. Collaboration with leading environmental researchers. Practical experience influencing conservation policy across Southern Africa. Graduates completing this research will also strengthen their prospects for careers in: Environmental consulting. Water resource management. Conservation organizations. Government agencies. International environmental organizations. Academic research. Doctoral studies. About Southern Waters Ecological Research and Consulting The research project is implemented by Southern Waters Ecological Research and Consulting , a specialist environmental consultancy working on freshwater ecosystems, ecological assessments, biodiversity conservation, and sustainable water management throughout Southern Africa. The organization partners with governments, conservation agencies, and research institutions to develop science-based solutions for protecting aquatic ecosystems. Conclusion The Stellenbosch University MSc Conservation Ecology Bursary 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity for aspiring environmental scientists to undertake fully funded postgraduate research while contributing to one of Southern Africa s most important freshwater conservation initiatives. By participating in the Water Research Commission s Free-Flowing Rivers project, the successful candidate will gain valuable research experience, advanced scientific training, and the opportunity to produce work that informs regional conservation policies and sustainable river management. South African graduates with backgrounds in ecology, hydrology, environmental science, or related fields are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit complete documentation before the 31 August 2026 deadline. Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Stellenbosch University MSc Conservation Ecology Bursary 2027: Fully Funded Master s Opportunity for South African Students to Advance Free-Flowing River Conservation Across the SADC Region appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UKRI What Works Innovation Fellowship 2026–2027: Join the Youth Endowment Fund to Help Prevent Violence Against Women and Girls
summary: The UKRI What Works Innovation Fellowship – What Works to Prevent Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG) is now accepting applications from outstanding researchers who are passionate about generating evidence that influences public policy and improves lives. Hosted by the Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) and funded by UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) , this prestigious 18-month fellowship offers researchers the opportunity to lead innovative projects that strengthen evidence on preventing violence against women and girls across the United Kingdom. The fellowship is one of three UKRI-funded What Works Innovation Fellowships and provides a unique opportunity to collaborate with government-backed What Works Centres, policymakers, researchers, and practitioners working to tackle one of society s most pressing challenges. If you are an experienced researcher with expertise in violence prevention, public policy, social research, or related disciplines, this fellowship offers the chance to shape national policy while contributing to cutting-edge research. Application Deadline: 8 September 2026 (16:00 UK Time) About the UKRI What Works Innovation Fellowship The fellowship forms part of the Research Development Missions Accelerator Programme funded by UK Research and Innovation (UKRI). Successful fellows will spend 18 months generating high-quality evidence, conducting innovative research, and supporting the launch of a National What Works Programme on Tackling Violence Against Women and Girls . The three fellowships are hosted by: Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) Foundations – The What Works Centre for Children Families College of Policing – What Works Centre for Crime Reduction This opportunity specifically focuses on the fellowship hosted by the Youth Endowment Fund (YEF). Fellowship Details Host Organisation Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) Funding Funded by UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) Location London, United Kingdom (Hybrid Working) Start Date 4 January 2027 Duration 18 months Interview Period Week commencing 28 September 2026 Application Deadline 16:00 (UK Time), Tuesday 8 September 2026 About the Youth Endowment Fund The Youth Endowment Fund was established in 2019 by children s charity Impetus , supported by a £200 million endowment from the UK Home Office . Its mission is to prevent children and young people from becoming involved in violence by identifying, testing, and promoting evidence-based interventions that make communities safer. Purpose of the Fellowship The selected fellow will support the Youth Endowment Fund in expanding the evidence base on preventing violence against women and girls (VAWG) , particularly among young people. The fellowship aims to strengthen national understanding of: Violence prevention strategies Evidence-based interventions Online harms affecting young people Technology-facilitated violence against women and girls Policy development Programme evaluation Research translation into practice The successful fellow will also contribute to the creation of a National What Works Programme focused on preventing violence against women and girls. What You Will Do As a What Works Innovation Fellow at the Youth Endowment Fund, your responsibilities may include: Lead Innovative Research Projects You will design and lead research projects that address critical evidence gaps relating to violence against women and girls. Particular focus areas include: Online abuse Technology-facilitated violence Youth violence Prevention interventions Emerging research priorities Analyse National Data You will collaborate with the Data and Insight Team to: Improve the annual Child Vulnerability and Violence Survey Analyse existing datasets Produce new evidence relevant to VAWG prevention Conduct Evidence Reviews The fellow will work closely with the YEF Toolkit Team to: Review international evidence Summarise research findings Produce practical guidance for practitioners and policymakers Review Current Practice You will examine violence prevention programmes across England and Wales to: Identify effective practices Inform programme development Improve evaluation frameworks Support Secondary Data Projects Responsibilities may also include: Managing commissioned research Supporting secondary data analysis Producing reports Translating findings into policy recommendations Collaborate Nationally The fellowship includes collaboration with: UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) Cabinet Office Evaluation Task Force Other What Works Centres Universities Violence Against Women and Girls sector organisations National research partners Benefits of the Fellowship Successful applicants will receive the opportunity to: Lead nationally significant research Work with one of the UK s leading evidence organisations Influence public policy Collaborate with government departments Build partnerships across academia and practice Strengthen research leadership skills Expand professional networks Produce impactful research outputs Help launch a national programme on preventing violence against women and girls Contribute to evidence that informs government decision-making Who Can Apply? Applicants must: Be employed by a UK organisation eligible for UKRI funding Hold a PhD or possess equivalent research experience Demonstrate strong methodological expertise Have relevant subject-matter expertise Be interested in violence prevention and evidence generation The fellowship welcomes researchers at all career stages . Ideal Candidate Profile Competitive applicants will demonstrate: Strong research experience Expertise in violence prevention or related fields Excellent analytical skills Ability to conduct independent research Strong written communication Experience working collaboratively Interest in translating evidence into policy Commitment to improving outcomes for young people Application Requirements Applicants must submit: A Curriculum Vitae (CV) An Application Statement Each document should not exceed three pages , for a maximum submission of six pages . Application Statement Questions Your application statement should address: 1. Vision Explain: What you hope to achieve during the fellowship Your research ambitions The impact you wish to create 2. Capability Describe: Why you are the right candidate Relevant expertise Research experience Subject knowledge 3. Approach Explain how you intend to: Co-design the fellowship with the Youth Endowment Fund Deliver meaningful research outcomes Collaborate effectively with stakeholders Important Dates Activity Date Applications Close 8 September 2026 (16:00 UK Time) Interviews Week commencing 28 September 2026 Fellowship Begins 4 January 2027 Duration 18 Months Why You Should Apply The UKRI What Works Innovation Fellowship offers far more than a traditional research position. Fellows become part of a national movement dedicated to improving evidence-informed policymaking around violence prevention. Working alongside leading researchers, government agencies, universities, and practitioners, fellows will contribute to meaningful societal change while developing high-level research leadership experience. If you are passionate about evidence, policy, social impact, and preventing violence against women and girls, this fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity to shape the future of violence prevention in the UK. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before 16:00 (UK Time) on Tuesday, 8 September 2026 . Apply Here For more information, visit official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post UKRI What Works Innovation Fellowship 2026–2027: Join the Youth Endowment Fund to Help Prevent Violence Against Women and Girls appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Lund University PhD in Sustainability Science 2027 in Sweden (Fully Funded with Salary): Apply for a Doctoral Research Position on Just Energy Transitions
summary: Lund University, one of Europe s oldest and most prestigious universities, is inviting applications for a PhD in Sustainability Science at the Lund University Centre for Sustainability Studies (LUCSUS) . This exciting doctoral opportunity offers successful applicants the chance to conduct cutting-edge interdisciplinary research on the role of finance in shaping just energy transitions while earning a full salary throughout the four-year doctoral programme. The position is based in Lund, Sweden , and is part of a prestigious FORMAS-funded international research project that explores how financial systems influence the transition away from fossil fuels. The successful candidate will work alongside researchers in Sweden and Canada while contributing to one of the world s most pressing sustainability challenges. Application Deadline: 28 August 2026 About Lund University Founded in 1666 , Lund University consistently ranks among the world s leading universities. With approximately 46,000 students and 8,500 staff , the university is internationally recognized for excellence in research and education. The successful PhD candidate will join the Lund University Centre for Sustainability Studies (LUCSUS) , a globally respected institute dedicated to addressing sustainability challenges through interdisciplinary research and innovative educational programmes. LUCSUS conducts research across several major sustainability themes, including: Climate change and resilience Land-use governance and development Urban governance and transformation Energy justice and sustainable energy systems Biodiversity and natural resource management The centre collaborates extensively with international partners and promotes both critical thinking and practical solutions to global sustainability issues. About the PhD Programme The Sustainability Science PhD programme is a 240-credit doctoral programme , equivalent to four years of full-time study . Unlike many doctoral programmes around the world, this position is a salaried employment opportunity. Successful candidates will: Receive a monthly salary from Lund University Conduct independent doctoral research Complete coursework Write and publicly defend a doctoral dissertation The appointment may also include teaching responsibilities of up to 20% of working time. If teaching duties are assigned, the doctoral appointment will be extended accordingly. The successful applicant will: Be based full-time in Lund, Sweden Receive office space at LUCSUS Become part of an international research community Collaborate with leading sustainability researchers Research Project: The Financial Sector s Role in Just Energy Transitions The doctoral research forms part of the FORMAS-funded project titled: (Dis)orderly Transitions: The Financial Sector s Role in Shaping Just Energy Transitions. The project builds upon the COP28 agreement , which emphasized the need for moving away from fossil fuels in a: Just Orderly Equitable manner. The research investigates an important question: How does the financial sector influence the global transition away from fossil fuels? Researchers will critically examine how financial institutions attempt to create orderly transitions through financial instruments that seek to manage uncertainty, risks, investments, and future pathways. The project combines perspectives from: Political economy Economic sociology Sustainability science Critical social sciences Research Focus The selected PhD researcher will conduct qualitative fieldwork involving financial actors across Scandinavia. Research topics include: Transition bonds Green taxonomies Financial markets Risk management Future-making practices Energy transitions Power dynamics within financial systems Fieldwork may include: Interviews Participant observation Document analysis Stakeholder engagement The research aims to improve understanding of how financial systems influence both the opportunities and inequalities emerging during energy transitions. What You ll Receive Successful applicants will benefit from numerous opportunities, including: Fully Paid Doctoral Position Candidates receive a monthly salary from Lund University throughout the four-year programme. Four-Year Employment The appointment covers the full duration of doctoral studies. International Research Collaboration Researchers will work with leading scholars in Sweden and Canada. Office Space Students receive dedicated workspace at LUCSUS. Teaching Opportunities Eligible candidates may gain valuable university teaching experience. Professional Development The programme provides: Advanced research training International networking Conference participation opportunities Academic publishing experience Collaboration with policymakers and researchers Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy the general entry requirements for doctoral education in Sweden. Applicants should have either: A second-cycle qualification (Master s degree) before 1 January 2027 , or At least 240 higher education credits , including 60 credits at second-cycle level , or Equivalent international qualifications. Required Qualifications Ideal candidates should possess: A Master s degree in Sustainability Science, Human Geography, Sociology, Political Science, Anthropology, Science and Technology Studies, or a closely related discipline Strong interest in critical social science Demonstrated interest in climate, energy, or finance research Experience conducting qualitative research Excellent written and spoken English Ability to work independently Ability to collaborate within interdisciplinary and international research teams Willingness to contribute to teaching and departmental activities Desired Qualifications Although not mandatory, the following strengthen an application: Swedish or another Scandinavian language skills Knowledge of political economy Economic sociology Social constructivist approaches Experience with interviews and discourse analysis Stakeholder mapping experience Experience engaging policymakers, NGOs, or investors Scientific publishing experience Applicants are encouraged to apply even if they meet only some of these desirable qualifications. Assessment Criteria Applicants will be evaluated based on: Academic excellence Independent research potential Motivation Communication skills English language proficiency Collaboration skills Suitability for interdisciplinary sustainability research Scandinavian language skills are advantageous but not required . Application Documents Applicants must submit the following electronically: 1. Cover Letter Maximum 2,000 words , including: Personal introduction Motivation Relevant qualifications Interest in the project Proposed research interests 2. CV Maximum 2 pages , including: Academic background Research experience Publications (if applicable) 3. Degree Certificates Academic transcripts and certificates. 4. Thesis Copies of thesis work totaling at least 30 credits , including: Master s thesis, or Master s and Bachelor s theses 5. Professional References Names and contact information for three professional referees . Reference letters are not required during the first application stage . Employment Information Institution: Lund University Department: Lund University Centre for Sustainability Studies (LUCSUS) Location: Lund, Sweden Employment Type: Temporary doctoral appointment Employment Level: Full-time (100%) Start Date: 1 January 2027 Salary: Monthly salaried position Number of Positions: One Why Apply? This PhD position offers far more than a doctoral degree. It provides the opportunity to: Conduct impactful sustainability research Receive a competitive university salary Work with internationally recognized scholars Investigate one of today s most significant climate and energy policy challenges Develop expertise in sustainability governance, finance, and energy transitions Join one of Europe s leading sustainability research institutions Apply Here For aspiring sustainability researchers interested in climate governance, political economy, finance, and just transitions, this represents an exceptional career opportunity. For more information, visit the official website. Discover more more global research opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Lund University PhD in Sustainability Science 2027 in Sweden (Fully Funded with Salary): Apply for a Doctoral Research Position on Just Energy Transitions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
